title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner. Runtime >= 20 min,
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety.
analysis: Key stakeholders and needs: (1) End users need adequate cleaning performance with at least 20 minutes usable runtime per charge, comfortable ergonomics under 1.5 kg total mass, acceptable noise/heat during indoor operation (10–40°C), safe and intuitive charging, and easy maintenance (emptying/filter cleaning). (2) Buyer/brand/product management needs a clear value proposition and compliance readiness for EU sale (CE marking), including defined performance metrics (runtime measured under specified mode/load) and manufacturable cost/quality targets. (3) Regulatory/compliance stakeholders need conformity with applicable EU directives/regulations (e.g., Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, and battery-related rules), plus harmonized standards selection and technical documentation for CE marking; safe battery/charger design, labeling, and instructions. (4) Manufacturing/supply chain needs component availability (battery cells, motor, charger), repeatable assembly meeting weight and reliability targets, and test procedures for end-of-line verification (runtime, charging safety, leakage current/insulation as applicable, EMC). (5) Service/support needs replaceable consumables (filters), diagnostic guidance, warranty assumptions, and safe handling guidance for batteries. Nonfunctional requirements to refine: runtime definition (mode, suction level, duty cycle), weight definition (including battery, accessories), charging requirements (time, connector/type, charger inclusion), and operating/environmental constraints (humidity, storage, drop/ingress protection).
clarification_questions: How should the >=20 min runtime be measured (which power mode/suction setting, with or without brush head, and what test method/soil load)?
Does the <1.5 kg weight limit include the battery and standard attachments (e.g., floor brush/crevice tool), and is there a maximum allowable center-of-mass/ergonomic constraint?
What charging requirements apply: target charge time, whether charger is in-box, and preferred interface (e.g., USB-C PD vs dedicated dock) and mains input range (EU 230V only or 100–240V)?

table.title: DFMEA - Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner (EU safety, runtime>=20 min, weight<1.5 kg)
rows: Battery pack (Li-ion cells + pack assembly)
Provide energy for >=20 min usable runtime per charge within 10–40°C
Insufficient runtime / rapid capacity fade
User cannot complete cleaning task; increased returns; brand dissatisfaction; potential warranty cost
CC
Cell capacity variation; high internal resistance; poor thermal management; high load profile; inaccurate runtime definition/use case mismatch
Cell supplier qualification; incoming cell IR/capacity screening; thermal design review; load profiling; BMS configuration limits
EOL runtime test under defined mode/load; pack capacity test sampling; field data monitoring
Tighten runtime spec (mode/load); improve airflow/motor efficiency; increase cell grade or capacity; add thermal path; implement pack-level capacity binning
Battery Systems Engineer
Planned
Battery pack (BMS/PCM protection)
Prevent overcharge/overdischarge/overcurrent; ensure safe operation and transport
BMS fails to cut off on overcharge
Cell venting/thermal runaway risk; fire hazard; regulatory noncompliance; injury/property damage
SC
Protection IC failure; wrong component values; charger fault; MOSFET short; PCB contamination; firmware/threshold error
Redundant OVP (secondary protector/thermal fuse); component derating; conformal coat; design FMEA/FTA; supplier PPAP
Pack OVP functional test; charge abuse test per standards; ICT; firmware parameter verification
Add secondary OVP device and thermal cutoff; strengthen DVP abuse testing; add automated calibration/parameter lock; enhance cleanliness controls
Safety Compliance Engineer
In progress
Battery pack (BMS/PCM protection)
Protect cells and maintain performance under high load
BMS nuisance trip / overcurrent threshold too low
Vacuum stops intermittently; perceived poor quality; user frustration; potential returns
CC
Threshold tolerance stack-up; inrush current; MOSFET heating; wiring resistance; motor stall events
Current profiling; soft-start control; wiring gauge spec; thermal simulation; tolerance analysis
EOL high-load start/stop test; stall simulation test; BMS log review (if available)
Implement motor soft-start; adjust OCP threshold with margin; improve conductors/connector; add stall detection to reduce peak current
Power Electronics Engineer
Planned
Motor (BLDC + driver)
Generate airflow at required suction with acceptable noise and efficiency
Motor overheating / coil insulation degradation
Loss of suction; reduced runtime; potential burn odor/smoke; safety risk; shortened life
SC
Blocked airflow; high duty cycle; poor heat sinking; incorrect commutation; bearing friction; overvoltage
Thermal design and airflow margin; temp sensor/derating; material class verification; motor supplier validation
Thermal chamber test at 40°C; stall/blocked inlet test; EOL current draw check
Add temperature-based power derating; improve motor cooling path; specify higher temp insulation class; add blockage detection and user alert
Motor Design Engineer
Planned
Motor bearings/rotor assembly
Maintain low friction and noise over life
Bearing wear / rotor imbalance leading to vibration
Excess noise; reduced suction efficiency; user discomfort; potential housing cracks over time
CC
Bearing contamination; inadequate lubrication; imbalance from manufacturing; drop damage
Supplier process control; balancing specification; clean assembly; drop robustness design
EOL vibration/noise test; incoming bearing audits; life test sampling
Tighten balancing tolerances; add vibration isolators; improve dust sealing to motor; increase life test duration and sample size
Supplier Quality Engineer
Planned
Suction path / nozzle / airflow channels
Deliver adequate debris pickup and airflow without clogging
Clogging at nozzle or internal duct
Sudden suction loss; overheating; reduced runtime; user dissatisfaction; maintenance burden
CC
Geometry pinch points; large debris ingestion; hair accumulation; insufficient cyclonic separation; rough internal surfaces
Airpath design rules; CFD; debris ingestion criteria; smooth surface finish spec; hair management features
Debris clog tests; airflow (CFM) EOL check; user trials
Redesign duct transitions; add anti-hair features and grate; specify minimum bend radius; include clear clog access; update user instructions
Mechanical Design Engineer
Planned
Filter (primary/HEPA element)
Capture fine dust while maintaining airflow; easy to clean/replace
Filter loads quickly / high pressure drop
Reduced suction and runtime; increased motor heat and noise; frequent maintenance; returns
CC
Insufficient filter area; low permeability media; poor pre-separation; user washes incorrectly; wet dust
Filter media selection; pre-filter/cyclone design; maintenance instructions; washability validation
Dust loading tests; airflow vs. loading characterization; EOL airflow test
Increase filter area; improve cyclonic stage; specify media with higher dust-holding capacity; add maintenance indicator; update cleaning guidance
Airflow/Filtration Engineer
Planned
Filter sealing interface
Prevent bypass leakage around filter
Filter bypass due to poor seal or misassembly
Dust enters motor; reduced life; fine dust emission; user health concerns; noncompliance claims
SC
Gasket compression set; tolerance stack-up; user installs incorrectly; latch weakness; warped housing
Poka-yoke filter fit; robust latch; gasket material selection; tolerance analysis; assembly work instructions
Leak test (pressure decay) sampling; visual inspection; user misuse testing
Add keyed filter geometry; improve gasket design/material; add positive latch feedback; introduce EOL leak test for 100% units
Quality Engineer
Planned
Housing/structure (plastic enclosure)
Provide structural integrity and ergonomic handling under drops; weight <1.5 kg
Housing crack after drop / impact
Exposure of live parts/battery; loss of containment; poor appearance; safety risk; warranty claims
SC
Material brittle at low temp; stress concentrators; insufficient ribs; screw boss failure; drop orientation extremes
Material selection with impact modifiers; rib/boss design guidelines; drop simulation; torque spec control
Drop tests on multiple orientations/temps; EOL visual inspection; torque audit
Increase toughness material; add ribs/fillets; redesign bosses; add internal energy-absorbing features; tighten assembly torque controls
Mechanical Reliability Engineer
Planned
Housing thermal interface / user touch points
Keep external surface temperature within safe/comfortable limits
Surface temperature too high during operation
User discomfort/burn risk; reduced perceived quality; potential safety nonconformance
SC
Poor heat spreading; motor/battery near grip; blocked vents; high ambient (40°C); prolonged high-power mode
Thermal layout review; insulation barriers; vent placement; power derating strategy
Thermal testing at max ambient; touch temperature measurements; user trial feedback
Reposition heat sources away from grip; add heat shield/spreader; implement temperature-based performance limit; improve venting while meeting ingress goals
Thermal Engineer
Planned
Charger (AC adapter/dock) - electrical safety
Charge battery safely; meet EU safety/EMC requirements
Output overvoltage / incorrect charger regulation
Battery damage; overheating/fire risk; noncompliance; injury/property damage
SC
Wrong adapter variant; component failure; poor feedback loop stability; counterfeit supply; labeling mismatch
Use certified adapter (EN/IEC 62368-1/61558 as applicable); supplier audits; unique connector; clear labeling; incoming inspection
Incoming functional test; OVP verification; safety certification review; EMC pre-scan
Lock approved charger P/N; add adapter ID/keying; tighten incoming test plan; add OVP clamp on device side; strengthen supplier anti-counterfeit controls
Electrical Safety Engineer
In progress
Charging connector / dock contacts
Provide reliable charging connection over life; safe touch
Intermittent contact / high contact resistance
Charging fails or slow; localized heating at contacts; user frustration; potential minor burn
CC
Wear/corrosion; contamination; weak spring force; misalignment; thin plating
Gold plating spec; contact force design; alignment features; ingress management; cycle testing
Charge cycle endurance test; EOL charge current verification; contact resistance measurement sampling
Increase plating thickness; improve self-aligning dock; add debris shields; specify higher spring force with fatigue margin; add 100% charge current check
Connector Design Engineer
Planned
UI (buttons/LED indicators)
Provide intuitive control and accurate status (battery, charging, faults)
Battery indicator inaccurate / misleading
User experiences unexpected shutdown; dissatisfaction; mischarging behavior; support calls/returns
CC
Poor SOC algorithm; cell aging not compensated; voltage sag under load; LED tolerance; firmware bug
SOC algorithm validation under load/aging; firmware reviews; calibration table; requirements definition for “usable runtime”
Software unit/integration tests; runtime-to-empty validation; EOL functional UI test
Implement load-compensated SOC and aging model; add low-battery pre-warning with hysteresis; improve UI labeling; extend validation across temps/cell lots
Embedded Software Engineer
Planned
UI (power switch / control logic)
Prevent unintended operation; safe start/stop behavior
Unintended start (button stuck or logic fault)
Injury risk; battery drain; overheating in confined space; compliance issue
SC
Button mechanical jam; liquid/dust ingress; debounce bug; ESD upset; transport activation
Button design with return force; ingress barriers; firmware debounce and long-press requirement; ESD design
ESD testing; abuse/contamination tests; EOL button functional test; transport simulation
Require long-press to start; add travel stop and sealing membrane; implement watchdog and state validation; add shipment lock mode
Systems Engineer
Planned
Seals (dustbin gasket, airpath O-rings)
Maintain airtight suction path and contain dust
Seal leak / gasket compression set over time
Reduced suction; dust leakage to environment; noise increase; user complaints; performance spec miss
CC
Wrong elastomer hardness; chemical exposure from cleaning; poor compression design; tolerance stack-up; repeated opening/closing
Material compatibility testing; compression set specification; groove design guidelines; tolerance analysis; user maintenance instructions
Leak/airflow test; life cycling of dustbin latch; environmental aging tests
Select lower compression-set material (e.g., silicone/EPDM as appropriate); redesign gland for correct squeeze; add stop features; introduce periodic replacement recommendation
Materials Engineer
Planned

messages.content: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety.

Key stakeholders and needs: (1) End users need adequate cleaning performance with at least 20 minutes usable runtime per charge, comfortable ergonomics under 1.5 kg total mass, acceptable noise/heat during indoor operation (10–40°C), safe and intuitive charging, and easy maintenance (emptying/filter cleaning). (2) Buyer/brand/product management needs a clear value proposition and compliance readiness for EU sale (CE marking), including defined performance metrics (runtime measured under specified mode/load) and manufacturable cost/quality targets. (3) Regulatory/compliance stakeholders need conformity with applicable EU directives/regulations (e.g., Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, and battery-related rules), plus harmonized standards selection and technical documentation for CE marking; safe battery/charger design, labeling, and instructions. (4) Manufacturing/supply chain needs component availability (battery cells, motor, charger), repeatable assembly meeting weight and reliability targets, and test procedures for end-of-line verification (runtime, charging safety, leakage current/insulation as applicable, EMC). (5) Service/support needs replaceable consumables (filters), diagnostic guidance, warranty assumptions, and safe handling guidance for batteries. Nonfunctional requirements to refine: runtime definition (mode, suction level, duty cycle), weight definition (including battery, accessories), charging requirements (time, connector/type, charger inclusion), and operating/environmental constraints (humidity, storage, drop/ingress protection).
